title: Research
text:
Tropical Socio-Ecology

Bacurú Drõa

Maximiliane Jousse, Salomon Aguilar, Hector Barrios, Davilio Chami, Daisy H. Dent, Iran Dogirama, Homilia Flaco, Matthias Kunz, Joseph Levitan, Edison Degaiza, Wiyan Valdespino, David Mitre, Edilberto Mosquera, Helene C. Muller-Landau, Alexis Ortega, Rolando Perez, Jorge Valdes, Juan-Carlos Villareal, Catherine Potvin (2025). Insights into the forests of Darién, Panama, from the new 10 ha Bacurú Drõa plot established through participatory methods within an Emberá territory. Conservation Science and Practice. https://doi.org/10.1111/csp2.7016



Carbon Accounting in a small community
Tropical forests store about one quarter of all terrestrial carbon and have the potential for re-creating carbon sinks through reforestation. The Bayano-McGill Reforestation Project, established in 2020, aims to offset part of the university’s carbon emissions through planting and maintaining reforested plots in two Embera communities: Piriati and Ipeti.
Indigenous communities are stewards, owners or have legally designated rights to about a quarter of the world’s global forest estate, more than a third of intact forest landscapes, and almost a fifth of forest carbon stocks. This project slots itself into a framework that aims to foster community engagement and individual scientific and environmental interest within the Embera communities of Lake Bayano Area.
This project thus aimed to create a sustainable and accessible way for participants of the project to monitor their reforested plots independently. A carbon calculator was created, and a 3-day workshop led to the creation of a protocol and documentation to be followed.
See my dedicated github repository!

title: Research
text:
Biodiversity Indicators
Biodiversity indicators are metrics developed to be used in biodiversity monitoring and policy to measure progress towards a goal or deviation from a historical baseline. These are widely used in the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework and associated reporting.

Assessing Indicators

Jessica Currie, Sarah M Ravoth, Valentina Marconi, Louise McRae, Maria I Arce-Plata, Sandra Emry, Robin Freeman, Maximiliane Jousse, Gaëlle Mével, Shuaishuai Li, Cristian A Cruz-Rodríguez, David AGA Hunt, Philippa Oppenheimer, Lauren Gill, Janaina Serrano, Stefanie Deinet (2026). Navigating methodological decisions: Balancing rigor and data volume of the Canadian Living Planet Index. FACETS. https://doi.org/10.1139/facets-2025-0112


Katherine Hébert, Maximiliane Jousse, Janaína Serrano, Dirk Nikolaus Karger, F. Guillaume Blanchet, Laura J. Pollock (2025). Five recommendations to fill the blank space in indicators at local and short-term scales. Biological Conservation. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.biocon.2025.111007



Recommending Indicators for Biodiversity Monitoring

Katherine Hébert, Dagoberto Hernandez Acevedo, Sabrina Courant, Caroline Daguet, Andrew Gonzalez, Dominique Gravel, Jean Huot, Maximiliane Jousse, Janaína Serrano, Anouk Simard, Laura Pollock (2025). title: Maximiliane Jousse
text:
Welcome to my website :) I am a Quantitative Life Sciences PhD Student in Dr. Laura Pollock’s Lab at McGill University in Montreal, Canada.
My PhD research centers on biotic interactions in macroecology. Specifically, i look at integrating trophic interactions in species distribution models to better model the effects of climate change on species distributions. For more information check out my Research tab!

title: Research
text:
AI And Ecology

Recommendations for AI use in Conservation
Artificial Intelligence regroups many different methods with wide ranging applications. In recent years, we see many exciting and potential applications of AI in ecology and conservation. In collaboration with Dr. Hebert and Dr. Pollock, I looked at potential applications of AI, as well as risks and benefits of implementing these methods in conservation organisations. Our work informed reports to Parks Canada and Environment Climate Change Canada.


Applied AI to Ecology
I am a student member of the Ai for Biodiversity Change (ABC) Global Center. This academic group aims to “develop new AI-enabled, data-supported approaches for understanding the impacts of environmental changes on biodiversity”.
As part of this group, I was part of research aiming to leverage different sensors and computer vision to monitor an ecosystem undergoing restoration. Additionally, I was part of a group looking at how best to optimize digitization of specimens to enable FAIR downstream uses.

title: Research
text:
Biotic Interactions

Species Distribution Modelling with Interactions
My current work looks at the effects of incorporating species interactions in Species Distribution Modelling. Specifically, i am interested in 1) how much does adding biotic interactions affect our modelling abilities, and 2) How the inclusion of biotic interactions change our predictions under climate change.


Biotic Interactions & Conservation
I am also interested in the interplay between species interactions and conservation challenges.

Maximiliane Jousse, Pierre Rogy, Laura J. Pollock. Biotic interactions in conservation decision making. Book chapter for Biotic Interactions across Gradients and Scales. In review.
